title: TargetResults
description: The published numbers to reproduce, one entry per target quantity, EACH tagged with a reproducibility class assigned by the Interpreter BEFORE any analysis runs. The Comparator scores agent output against these using the pre-assigned class. This file is written once by the Interpreter and is immutable for the rest of the run - if the Diagnoser thinks a class is wrong, it says so in diagnosis.md; it does not edit this file.

table1_targets.description: Baseline characteristics to match FIRST (a cohort that doesn't match theirs is why the HR won't).

endpoint.description: Which endpoint this belongs to, or 'table1' / 'cohort'.

quantity.description: What is being measured, e.g. 'adjusted HR for age >=70 vs <60' or 'median OS' or '1-year NRM CIF'.

expected.description: The published value. May be null with a TODO note if the Interpreter has not yet read the exact figure from the PMC text - but a value MUST be present before the Comparator runs.

source_location.description: Where in the paper, e.g. 'Table 2, row age>=70' - for auditability.

class_reason.description: WHY this class, grounded in the methods text and the data dictionary. Required. E.g. 'within-tolerance: adjusted HR from Cox; all adjustment covariates present in public file' or 'coarsening-limited: continuous-age effect, but public file bins age to categories'.

expected_divergence_direction.description: For coarsening-limited targets: which way we expect the divergence to go, if predictable. Lets the Comparator verify the divergence BEHAVES as predicted instead of just failing.
